items.title: Loss functions and outlier leverage
items.description: Squared-error (MSE) losses amplify large residuals, so outliers in targets or features can dominate training; Huber and other robust losses down-weight extremes after a threshold.

items.title: Linear regression sensitivity
items.description: Ordinary least squares minimizes MSE and has no built-in outlier resistance; a single high-leverage point can tilt the fitted line unless you clip, remove, or switch to robust regression.

items.title: Scaling and z-scores
items.description: Z-score outlier detection reuses the same mean-and-standard-deviation machinery as standardization; fit location and scale on training data only, as with scalers.

items.title: Normal distribution and the ±3σ rule
items.description: The common |z| > 3 threshold is motivated by Gaussian tails; skewed or heavy-tailed distributions violate that intuition, which is why IQR is often safer.
